steps.title: Fastest path: note the sphere facts first
steps.content: Use the sphere facts first: the diameter determines the radius, and the sphere volume formula connects the radius to volume before density gives mass.

steps.title: Identify the relationship and radius
steps.content: Mass equals density times volume. The diameter is $29.000$ cm, so the radius is $r=\frac{29}{2}=14.5$ cm. Use density $2.8$ g/cm³ and $\pi=3.14159$.

steps.title: Calculate sphere volume
steps.content:
First cube the radius: $(14.5)^3=3{,}048.625$. Then apply the sphere volume formula:
$$V=\frac{4}{3}(3.14159)(3{,}048.625)\approx12{,}770.04$$

steps.title: Calculate the mass
steps.content:
Multiply by the density:
$$2.8(12{,}770.04)\approx35{,}756$$
The mass is 35,756 grams.
